p.m: \displaystyle{\begin{aligned} 8 \amp = 1 -7(m-8) \amp 8 \amp = 1 -7(m-8) \\ 8 \amp = 1 +(-7) \cdot (m+(-8)) \amp 8\mathbf{{}-1} \amp = 1 -7(m-8)\mathbf{{}-1} \\ 8 \amp = 1 +(-7) \cdot m+(-7) \cdot (-8) \amp 7 \amp = -7(m-8) \\ 8 \amp = 1 -7m +56 \amp \frac{7}{-7} \amp = \frac{-7(m-8)}{-7} \\ 8 \amp = -7m +(1+56) \amp -1 \amp = m-8 \\ 8 \amp = -7m +57 \amp -1\mathbf{{}+8} \amp = m-8\mathbf{{}+8} \\ 8\mathbf{{}-57} \amp = -7m +57\mathbf{{}-57} \amp 7 \amp = m \\ -49 \amp = -7m \amp m \amp = 7 \\ \frac{-49}{-7} \amp = \frac{-7m}{-7} \\ 7 \amp = m\\ m \amp = 7 \end{aligned} }

solution.p: The solution to this equation is 7. To stress that this is a value assigned to m, some report m = 7. We can also say that the solution set is \{7\}, or that m\in\left\{7\right\}. If we substitute 7 in for m in the original equation 8=1-7\mathopen{}\left(m-8\right), the equation will be true. Please check this on your own; it is an important habit.
